public_reputation: Known around Plume’s End Outpost through day-to-day work as patrol leader.
appearance: Veyra Sunweave usually presents in bright-plumed coat brushed too often for comfort, with details that make their patrol leader status legible before introductions begin.
manner: Keeps road danger legible through habit, posture, and immediate attention to anything that looks one decision away from trouble. In Plume’s End Outpost, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of patrol leader in and around Plume’s End Outpost, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Plume’s End Outpost workable through the obligations of patrol leader while managing the immediate pressure of caravan disruption rumors, celestial enforcer sightings.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of patrol leader credible while absorbing the strain created by caravan disruption rumors, celestial enforcer sightings.

adventure_hooks.summary: Veyra Sunweave can draw the party into a locality problem centered on patrol leader duties in Plume’s End Outpost.
Veyra Sunweave becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with caravan disruption rumors, celestial enforcer sightings around Plume’s End Outpost.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe. V33_1 additive depth pass preserved canon while strengthening at-table identity, appearance, and social motive.
public_signal: In public, Veyra Sunweave is immediately readable as a patrol leader tied to Plume’s End Outpost.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Plume’s End Outpost: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Veyra Sunweave presents as someone shaped by the duties of a patrol leader and the daily pressures of Plume’s End Outpost.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a patrol leader, and proof that strangers will not make Plume’s End Outpost harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Veyra Sunweave refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a patrol leader too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can provide assignments, road intelligence, and quick practical aid beyond town walls.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Veyra Sunweave adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Veyra Sunweave always knows more about Plume’s End Outpost than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a patrol leader in Plume’s End Outpost.
